description: Phishing templates impersonating government agencies and regulatory bodies

templates.name: Better Business Bureau Complaint
suggested_subject_lines: BBB: Complaint filed against your organization
Better Business Bureau: Response required within 14 days
Formal complaint received — your response is needed
templates.notes: Best for managers and business owners. Complaint + regulatory authority is a powerful combination.

templates.name: FBI/Law Enforcement Crime Report
suggested_subject_lines: Your information is referenced in an active investigation
Law Enforcement: Document request regarding your account
FBI IC3: Cybercrime report filed using your information
templates.notes: High-anxiety authority lure invoking law enforcement. Powerful, but use with care — the fear factor can cause undue stress, so favor it for mature programs with strong follow-up education. Most credible when the sender domain mimics a known agency.

templates.name: FDIC Banking Survey
suggested_subject_lines: FDIC: Required banking survey — response mandatory
Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation: Customer verification
FDIC Compliance: Complete your required annual verification
templates.notes: Authority-and-compliance lure aimed at banking and finance staff; the 'mandatory survey' framing leverages regulatory pressure. The lower click rate reflects its narrower, more skeptical audience — pair it with finance-specific training.

templates.name: IRS Tax Refund Notification
suggested_subject_lines: IRS: Internal Revenue Service — Electronic Refund Notification
IRS Refund: Tax refund balance update for {{.Email}}
IRS Tax Notice: Review tax refund status on your account
templates.notes: Extremely effective during Q1 and Q2 tax season. The promise of an unexpected refund is a high-motivation trigger.
